tags.name: Cnoicín an tSeabhaic
tags.note: Irish summit data imported from mountainviews.ie
tags.source:name: www.mountainviews.ie/mv/index.php?mtnindex=1048

tags.description:
Beann - a grammatical form of Binn - peak 
Sidheán (an tSídheáin )- (of) fairy (Sí) mound
Fairy here means "na daoine maithe"/ "good people" not type with wings! Much feared and respected in Ireland, they were usually referred to indirectly in placenames.
tags.name: Beann a' tSídheáin
Carraig a' Rince
tags.source: Updated placename given by LC and JB

tags.description:
Said to be named after an English forester named Throunce who planted much of the wood from seed.
Spelling taken from  
The Schools’ Collection, Volume 0298, Page 219
tags.name: Carraig Throunce
Piceán
tags.note: According to Lankford this translates to "The Peak In The Hill"
tags.source_ref: A Collection Of Place Names From Cork County - Éamon Lankford

tags.description:
Suí - Seat
Finn - Finn (mac Cumhaill) 
This is name of the point on top of the hill. Also spelt as Cifín.
tags.name: Suí Finn
tags.source: survey;Given as Sifín by Lankford;Given as Suí Finn by SOL
tags.source_ref: A Collection Of Place Names From Cork County - Éamon Lankford

tags.description:
Carraig - Rock
a' - (of) the
Shleáin - Sleán/(Turf Spade)
This hill was supposed to look like a sleán from the South/South west. Shape has changed now due to development.
tags.name: Carraig a' Shleáin
tags.source: Name and meaning given by JB

tags.name: Péicín Cnoc Droim Fionn
tags.source: Given as Peicin Cnuic DruimFhinne by Boyle Sommerville

tags.description:
Carraig - rock
an (of) the
Ghealladóra - Dictionary lists gealladóir = gealltóir which is pledger or undertaker or promisor. MOS on his map has seining in brackets next to Ghealladóra. Suggesting it was a vantage point for leader of a pair of seine boats.
tags.name: Carraig a Néalladóra
tags.source: MOS Map of Reen;local knowledge;survey;Listed in Lankford as Carraig a Néalladóra. Néalladoir was a man man who looks out from the front of the seine-boat for traces of fish, and gives directions in making a haul

tags.description:
This is the highest point in Myross, Stuaicín can be interpreted as a small height or a spire.
Also know as Stookeen in english
tags.name: Stuaicín
tags.source_ref: A Collection Of Place Names From Cork County - Éamon Lankford

tags.name: Cnoc Sheáin de Barra
tags.source_ref: A Collection Of Place Names From Cork County - Éamon Lankford

tags.description: This steep section of road was known as Leaca an Chnoic (the hill slope)
tags.name: Leaca an Chnoic
